channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Minnesota’s economic forecast is today. Here are things to watch
item.description: The key economic report will be released Thursday, though it probably won’t get into full detail about how federal changes could impact Minnesota’s economy.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Klobuchar, Smith call for answers about potential Indian Health Service office lease termination
item.description: U.S. Senators Tina Smith and Amy Klobuchar are pressing for answers regarding the potential termination of the Bemidji Area Indian Health Service’s lease, underlining treaty obligations and concerns for disruptions in health care services.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Olson, MPR News

item.title: Veterans fired from federal jobs say they feel betrayed, including some who voted for Trump
item.description: Nathan Hooven is a disabled Air Force veteran who voted for Donald Trump in November. Barely three months later, he's now unemployed and says he feels betrayed by the president’s dramatic downsizing of the federal government that cost him his job.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk comes to Capitol Hill to meet with Republicans who discuss turning DOGE cuts into law
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Billionaire Elon Musk arrived on Capitol Hill Tuesday and learned about something new — budget rescissions, an obscure legislative tool that could bring legal heft to his federal budget slashing effort and enshrine the cuts into law.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: USAID head Peter Marocco tells lawmakers he's eyeing potential criminal charges for foreign aid fraud: report
item.description: The head of USAID told lawmakers Wednesday his staff was preparing potential criminal referrals against those who allegedly misused taxpayer dollars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nevada Democrats urge Lombardo to hire fired federal workers
item.description: Democratic leaders in the Nevada Assembly are urging Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo to hire federal workers laid off as part of recent firings from President Donald Trump and Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: State of Democra-Z: Three young voters weigh in on Trump’s first weeks in office
item.description: After President Trump’s address to Congress Tuesday night, MPR News host Nina Moini checks in with three college-age voters with differing political views.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: State officials forecast higher energy prices because of Trump tariffs
item.description: Minnesota is heavily reliant on Canada for imports of natural gas, crude oil and electricity, all of which may be subject to a 10 percent tariff.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Democrats, protesters gather at DOGE's 'ground zero' to protest spending cuts
item.description: Democratic lawmakers gathered outside the Office of Personnel Management to protest the Trump administration's efforts to cut federal costs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Inside Elon Musk's huddle with GOP senators: DOGE head touts $4M savings per day
item.description: DOGE head Elon Musk met with senators at the Capitol on Wednesday to update them on his progress and answer questions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Veterans are speaking out on the Trump administration's plans to cut the VA's budget
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Stephen Watson served in the Marines for 22 years and receives care through the Department of Veterans Affairs for a traumatic brain injury. He supports President Donald Trump and adviser Elon Musk's cost-cutting program — even if it affects the VA.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspect in Kabul airport bombing during Afghanistan pullout has been captured and is due in US court
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A suspected senior planner in the suicide bombing at the Kabul airport that killed 13 American service members and roughly 170 Afghan civilians during the chaotic withdrawal from Afghanistan has been captured and will appear Wednesday in federal court in the United States to face c
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: San Diego Congress member pushes Trump for Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: A San Diego congressional representative is pressing the Norwegian committee who awards the prestigious Nobel Prizes to consider President Donald Trump to be the 2025 recipient of the peace prize.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Divided SCOTUS rejects Trump administration's push to rebuke judge over foreign aid freeze
item.description: By a 5-4 vote Wednesday, the Supreme Court told U.S. District Judge Amir Ali to clarify his earlier order that required the Republican administration to release nearly $2 billion in aid for work that had already been done.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Democrats behavior presented party as 'hateful': Bill O'Reilly
item.description: Bill O'Reilly says the behavior from the Democrats was 'hateful' as they did not acknowledge things presented by Trump.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Read NPR’s annotated fact check of President Trump’s address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump vowed to keep up his campaign of “swift and unrelenting action” in reorienting the nation’s economy, immigration and foreign policy as he delivered an unyielding address Tuesday night to Congress and the nation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press and NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: 6 takeaways from Trump’s pointedly partisan address to Congress
item.description: After a firehose of a first six weeks back in the White House, President Trump delivered a boastful and partisan address to a joint session of Congress Tuesday night. Here are six takeaways from the speech.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Trump vowed to end surprise medical bills. The team working on that just got slashed
item.description: Trump's first round of staff cuts to federal agencies eliminated dozens of positions at the Center for Consumer Information and Insurance Oversight, in charge of implementing the No Surprises Act.
item.dc:creator: Noam Levey, MPR News

item.title: Dan Goldman looks to be 'the man in the arena' against Trump
item.description: Rep. Dan Goldman (D-N.Y.) wasn’t planning to run for Congress after his stint as the lead counsel on President Trump’s first impeachment.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: The IRS is drafting plans to cut as much as half of its 90,000-person workforce, AP sources say
item.description: The layoffs are part of the Trump administration’s efforts to shrink the size of the federal workforce through billionaire Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: House Republicans advised to avoid in-person town halls amid protests
item.description: House Republicans are being encouraged to avoid in-person town halls in favor of phone and livestreamed versions, advice that comes after constituents and activists rocked recent GOP events by expressing outrage about the Trump administration’s dramatic moves to reshape the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: As Trump shrinks other parts of government, immigration task forces grow
item.description: The Trump administration is tapping several other agencies to help deport and arrest those without legal status — a novel step that is prompting some pushback.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo and Chiara Eisner, MPR News

item.title: Wall Street falls again as losses wipe out all post-election gains for the S&P 500
item.description: Stocks tumbled on Wall Street Tuesday as a trade war between the U.S. and its key trading partners escalated and threatened to wipe out all the gains for the S&P 500 since Election Day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Zelenskyy's White House meeting with Trump, Vance turns to shouting match
item.description: A White House meeting that was intended to kick off negotiations over a deal that would offer the United States access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals unraveled Friday afternoon as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy clashed with President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance in the Oval Of
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, ZEKE MILLER and JUSTIN SPIKE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
